title: Write a Prometheus Config
description:
## Configure Metric Scraping

Prometheus is configured in YAML: a **`global`** section (e.g. `scrape_interval`) and a **`scrape_configs`** list of scrape jobs.

**Your task:** write **`/root/prometheus.yml`** that:
1. has a **`global`** section with a **`scrape_interval`**
2. has a **`scrape_configs`** list with a job named **`prometheus`** that scrapes a target on **`localhost:9090`**

> 💡 Validate the YAML with `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('/root/prometheus.yml'))"`.

hints.title: Prometheus config skeleton
hints.body: Set a global scrape_interval, then list scrape jobs; each job has a job_name and static targets.
hints.command:
printf 'global:
  scrape_interval: 15s

scrape_configs:
  - job_name: prometheus
    static_configs:
      - targets:
          - localhost:9090
' > /root/prometheus.yml

validation.description: Checks for a global scrape_interval and a 'prometheus' scrape job.

commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/prometheus.yml')); print('ok' if 'scrape_interval' in (d.get('global') or {}) else 'no')"
python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/prometheus.yml')); jobs=[j.get('job_name') for j in (d.get('scrape_configs') or [])]; print('ok' if 'prometheus' in jobs else 'no')"
